Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Pursuant To Ccp Sections 583.410 And 583.420
Off Calendar Oct-30-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Demian Oksenendler, Esq. 415-733-1050 of Mannion Lowe & Oksenendler for Plaintiff.
Dean Burnick, Esq. 925-937-9990 of Morison Prough LLP for Defendant RLI Ins. Co.
Leighton Koberlein, Esq. of Hansen Kohls Sommer & Jacob LLP for Defendant Woodruff-Sawyer, Cam Dickinson et al. through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares its views, Plaintiff argues clients will suffer extreme prejudice if the tenative rulings are adopted. Defendants' Counsel responds. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT WOODRUFF-SAWYER & CO.'S Motion To Dismiss Pursuant To Ccp Sections 583.410 And 583.420.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. The Court has the discretion to dismiss an action for delay in prosecution if (1) service is not made within two years after the action is commenced against the defendant or (2) the action is not brought to trial within three years after the action is commenced against the defendant. (Code Civ. Proc. 583.410, 583.420(a)(1),(2)(A).) Both grounds apply here, and Plaintiffs have failed to make a showing of excusable delay sufficient to justify their delay.

(see word minutes for full details)

An order is signed for all three motions. (see scanned order for details)
